description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, reinforced by three prominent metallic hoops encircling its body. The wood staves are vertically aligned and show subtle seams and grain, giving the barrel a rustic, aged appearance. The metallic bands are evenly spaced, with one at the top, one at the bottom, and one around the middle, providing structural integrity. The top opening is wide and unobstructed, revealing a hollow interior, and there is no visible lid or cover. The surface of the wood is matte with a natural brown tone, while the metal hoops have a dull, silvery-gray finish. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near workshops, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A wooden barrel with three metallic hoops, a wide open top, and a rustic, aged appearance. The wood is brown and matte, while the metal bands are silvery-gray.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, three metal hoops, and the open top.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same visible hoops and open top.
description_view_2: Rear view, mirroring the front, with all three metal bands and wood grain visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the open top and all three metal hoops.
